prompt.content:
Use file “Cards_Patient 13” to access the entire medical history / record for the patient. Please use the patient tab as the source of truth for all demographic information. Disregard patient identifiers (Name, Age, DOB, MRN) found within the text of the clinicalnotes tab as they have been shifted for de-identification purposes

----

Patient 13 visits her clinic appointment on 3rd January 2026. She reports dysuria and fever. What are the best antimicrobials to start?  Provide an explanation.

rubrics.name: States the patient has a history of recurrent urinary tract infections (UTI). (ver_b87ee9cdd57d42f3af199d266a4d6481)

golden_responses.golden_text_response: The patient has a history of recurrent urinary tract infections, and a urine culture is indicated. The patient has a recent urine culture. It is important to guide treatment with a prior urine culture. The urine culture grows *Stenotrophomonas maltophilia* and demonstrates susceptibility to nitrofurantoin, trimethoprim sulfamethoxazole (TMP SMX), and imipenem. The patient has not yet received antimicrobial therapy, so this current UTI has not yet been treated. One treatment option is combination therapy with at least two active agents, such as cefiderocol, minocycline, TMP SMX, or levofloxacin, listed in order of preference, at least until clinical improvement is observed, given the limited supportive data for any single agent. Alternatively, the combination of ceftazidime avibactam and aztreonam may be used, although clinical data supporting this regimen are limited. The urine culture demonstrates resistance to levofloxacin. Therefore, the combination of cefiderocol and minocycline represents the next best step in treatment.
